description:
Enable linking to aims in other locally-checked-out repositories. Settled design (2026-06):

DIRECTION & OWNERSHIP: A repo may only declare edges where a LOCAL aim is the supported (parent) side and the EXTERNAL aim is the supporter (child). You can always declare what supports you; never that another repo needs you. So a cross-repo edge is a supportingConnection on a local parent whose target lives in another repo. Weight stays on the local side. Value flows OUT of this repo into the external supporter. The external supporter keeps NO back-reference (supportedAims stays local-only).

IDENTITY: edges store a two-part key {repoId, aimId}. repoId defined ⇒ external. Registry is split: portable part (repoId, url, name) committed in meta.json; machine-local part (localPath, read/write access toggle) in .bowman/runtime/ (gitignored). Resolution is two-stage: edge {repoId,aimId} → machine-local repoId→localPath map → load. Unresolved ⇒ 'repo not checked out here' state, not a broken edge.

VALUE (model b): value is a conserved flow, not an aggregate. Merged calc = local aims (real intrinsic) + referenced external aims with intrinsicValue forced to 0 + cross-repo edges, fed to the existing calculateAimValues. Externals receive value ONLY from inflow; local values shrink as flow is exported (intended). totalIntrinsic stays = local intrinsics. v1: external linked aims are leaf sinks; cascade into their subtree only when expanded.

ROOTS: keep N root aims (no single synthetic project-root). Cross-repo edges always target a specific aim (often a central intrinsic-bearing one). No 'link the whole repo' sentinel.

RENDERING: partial/lazy in general — unloaded aims show as dotted stubs with no info; click to load + reveal relatives as dotted. Externals additionally tinted by their repo color and read-only by default.

WRITABLE EXTERNALS (deferred, independent): per-linked-repo machine-local read/write toggle; backend writeAim can target any local .bowman; sharing/pushing is plain git done by the user.

SEARCH: the aim-search modal (the universal connection-creation funnel) gets a repo scope selector (this/all/checkboxes), fans out to each linked repo's search, merges ranked results (same embedding model ⇒ comparable cosine), tags each by repo.
